subject: WM-0073 review: changes_requested
body: High: previous manifest-declared maxFileBytes/maxTotalBytes repair is present for honest byte measurements, but validateM5ContentPack still trusts caller-provided M5ContentPackFile.byteLength instead of deriving or cross-checking bytes from file.text. A semantic-valid probe with manifest maxFileBytes=1 and maxTotalBytes=10, actual text total 4856 bytes, and every file byteLength spoofed to 1 returned ok:true with counters.byteCount=7 and no diagnostics. This violates WM-0073 file-size/oversized-input fail-closed acceptance for the exported validation entry point. Compute actual bytes from text for validation or reject mismatched byteLength metadata, then add a regression for spoofed byteLength under manifest-declared caps.
